preamble: Diphthongization of [ɛ] to [ei] typically involves the raising of the target or the addition of a high front off-glide.

examples.note: Modern English 'take' historically moved from [a:] to [e:] and then to the modern [ei] or [eɪ] diphthong, often starting from an open-mid position.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
